title: Cyclone Early Warning And Evacuation Signal Verification In Bangladesh
summary: Build a verified workflow for assessing where cyclone track forecasts, storm surge models, and population exposure data can improve evacuation decision timing in coastal Bangladesh without encouraging false-confidence evacuation orders.
why_it_matters: Bangladesh reduced cyclone mortality from hundreds of thousands to dozens through its Cyclone Preparedness Programme, early warning systems, and cyclone shelters. But the gap between a forecast track and a verified evacuation signal remains — false alarms erode trust, and late warnings leave people in the storm path. Verifying which signals are trustworthy at which lead times can strengthen a proven life-saving system.

success_metrics.target: Every evacuation signal candidate links to a dated evidence record with method and limitations.
success_metrics.validation_method: Run schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against evidence.json and evidence.md.

success_metrics.target: Every signal candidate includes false-alarm rate by lead time as a primary metric.
success_metrics.validation_method: Review back-test results and confirm false-alarm documentation.

success_metrics.target: No evacuation trigger threshold is merged without domain review, replication, and field-reality review.
success_metrics.validation_method: Check task status, review records, and field-reality review sign-off.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication for all quantitative outputs, and field-reality review before any operational claim.
